exam.name: Elementary Education: Content Knowledge for Teaching
exam.category_description: Exam-aligned prep for future elementary educators with complete, syllabus-mapped coverage across reading, math, science, and social studies pedagogy.
exam.hero_headline: Pass Praxis 7811 with classroom-accurate exam coverage
exam.hero_subheadline: Master every tested subtest in focused 15-minute topics mapped directly to the official ETS Study Companion.
exam.companion_problem: Most Praxis prep is either too generic or fragmented, so candidates struggle to prioritize what is truly test-relevant.
exam.companion_solution: We convert the official ETS syllabus into a complete topic map with retention-focused notes and consistent concept coverage.
exam.learner_profile: Aspiring elementary teacher focused on Content Knowledge for Teaching - understanding not just what to teach but how students learn it. Responds to student-misconception scenarios, pedagogical examples, and instructional decision-making.

sections.title: Reading and Language Arts
sections.description: Essential content knowledge and specialized pedagogical understanding needed for teaching elementary reading and language arts.

topics.title: Print Concepts
topics.description: Understanding the features of print and how written words communicate messages.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate knowledge that written words communicate a message, use spaces, and follow a particular direction.
Recognize distinguishing features of sentences like capitalization and punctuation.
Differentiate between pictures and printed words on a page.

topics.title: Alphabetic Principle
topics.description: Understanding that print represents the sounds of spoken words.
learning_objectives: Identify uppercase and lowercase letter names, shapes, and corresponding sounds.
Demonstrate that phonemes are represented by graphemes in words.
Analyze and synthesize letter-sound relationships in decoding and encoding.

topics.title: Phonological Awareness
topics.description: Detecting and manipulating the sound structures of spoken language.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate understanding that speech comprises various phonological units.
Detect and manipulate parts of compound words and syllables.
Detect and manipulate onset-rime and individual phonemes.

topics.title: Phonics and Word Recognition
topics.description: Decoding unfamiliar words using grade-appropriate phonics and word-analysis skills.
learning_objectives: Pronounce unfamiliar words by applying letter-sound correspondences and orthographic patterns.
Read multisyllabic words using syllabication and identifying affixes.
Identify grade-appropriate, high-frequency sight words.

topics.title: Reading Fluency
topics.description: Reading text orally and silently with accuracy, rate, and expression.
learning_objectives: Read grade-level text with accuracy, appropriate rate, and prosody.
Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding.
Demonstrate sufficient stamina to finish reading tasks.

topics.title: Conventions of Standard Academic English
topics.description: Applying grammar, capitalization, punctuation, and spelling rules in oral and written discourse.
learning_objectives: Apply structural rules governing clauses, phrases, and word tenses.
Follow capitalization and punctuation conventions, including titles and commas.
Produce simple, compound, and complex sentences.
Spell grade-appropriate words applying orthographic patterns and morphology.

topics.title: Vocabulary Acquisition and Use
topics.description: Understanding denotative and connotative meanings and strategies to determine word meaning.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate knowledge of denotative and connotative meanings, including figurative language.
Clarify meanings of unknown words using context clues and word parts (affixes, roots).
Make word associations using antonyms, synonyms, and cognates.

topics.title: Forms and Functions of Language
topics.description: Understanding how language conventions, dialects, and stylistic choices affect meaning.
learning_objectives: Discern appropriate levels of formal language across contexts and analyze dialects/registers.
Select words, phrases, and punctuation for rhetorical effect and precision.
Make choices to expand, reduce, and combine sentences for meaning and interest.

topics.title: Key Ideas and Details in Text
topics.description: Reading closely to determine explicit meaning, make inferences, and cite textual evidence.
learning_objectives: Determine central ideas or themes and summarize key supporting details.
Recount stories and determine central messages, lessons, or morals.
Identify relationships within texts between characters, settings, events, or ideas.

topics.title: Author's Craft and Text Structure
topics.description: Analyzing how authors use language, structure, and text features to convey meaning.
learning_objectives: Analyze how printed language and specific word choice convey meaning and tone.
Describe overall text structure (cause/effect, problem/solution, sequence).
Use text features like captions and tables of contents to locate information.
Analyze craft and structure across texts to compare point of view and argument structures.

topics.title: Integration and Application of Knowledge
topics.description: Evaluating arguments and synthesizing information across various texts and media.
learning_objectives: Critique the validity of arguments and the relevance of evidence.
Integrate information across multiple texts to synthesize ideas or compare author approaches.
Explain how illustrations and visual representations support reader understanding.

topics.title: Narrative and Expository Text Types
topics.description: Understanding typical elements of genres and conventional text structures.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate knowledge of elements in narrative, procedural, and persuasive genres.
Use transitional words to link ideas across all text types.
Write narratives using sensory details and clear event sequencing.
Write expository texts with clear introductions, supporting facts, and logical organization.

topics.title: Production of Written Texts
topics.description: Adapting writing style to purpose and navigating the stages of the writing process.
learning_objectives: Produce clear and coherent writing adapted to audience, task, and purpose.
Navigate the writing process through planning, drafting, and revising stages.
Produce first-draft, on-demand, and extended writing.

topics.title: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
topics.description: Conducting research and gathering reliable evidence to support analysis.
learning_objectives: Locate, select, and categorize relevant information from different texts.
Analyze and reflect on evidence found in narrative and informational texts.
Determine the credibility, accuracy, and biases of sources.

topics.title: Discussion and Collaboration
topics.description: Participating effectively in conversations using discourse conventions.
learning_objectives: Use social knowledge of discourse conventions to communicate persuasively.
Ask and answer questions to gain deeper understanding in group discussions.
Paraphrase texts and integrate evidence into discussion arguments.
Reflect on and evaluate others' perspectives.

topics.title: Presentation of Knowledge and Ideas
topics.description: Organizing and presenting information appropriately for audience and purpose.
learning_objectives: Sequence ideas logically and establish a clear line of reasoning.
Speak clearly at an understandable pace, adopting suitable registers.
Use digital and visual media strategically to enhance expression of ideas.

sections.title: Mathematics
sections.description: Essential content knowledge and specialized pedagogical understanding needed for teaching elementary mathematics.

topics.title: Counting Concepts
topics.description: Foundational skills for counting, cardinality, and numerical relationships.
learning_objectives: Count and skip count whole numbers between 0 and 1,000.
Connect counting to cardinality and understand one-to-one correspondence.
Subitize small quantities by sight and relate counting to larger/smaller set sizes.

topics.title: Operations with Whole Numbers
topics.description: Representing and solving problems with addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate understanding of representations of the four operations using manipulatives and diagrams.
Solve mathematical and real-world problems involving the four operations.
Solve problems by using properties of operations.

topics.title: Place Value and Decimals
topics.description: Conceptual understanding of digit values, groupings, and base-ten representations.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate a conceptual understanding of the value of digits in a number.
Compose and decompose multidigit numbers into groupings.
Use drawings and manipulatives to represent place value and relate to numerical equations.

topics.title: Comparing and Rounding Numbers
topics.description: Evaluating magnitudes and estimating using multidigit and decimal numbers.
learning_objectives: Compare multidigit and decimal numbers using place value concepts.
Round multidigit and decimal numbers.
Explain why grouping and ungrouping are helpful in rounding and comparing.

topics.title: Fractions as Numbers and Equivalent Fractions
topics.description: Understanding part-whole relationships, unit fractions, and equivalence.
learning_objectives: Understand fractions as part-whole relationships, multiples of unit fractions, numbers, and ratios.
Demonstrate understanding of equipartitioning as a building block for fractions.
Demonstrate understanding of fraction equivalence.

topics.title: Comparing and Operating with Fractions
topics.description: Strategies for comparing magnitudes and calculating with fractions.
learning_objectives: Use a variety of strategies to compare fractions (less than, equal to, greater than one).
Perform addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with fractions and whole numbers.
Build intuition on operations, such as recognizing that multiplying by a fraction less than one reduces the product.

topics.title: Fraction Operations and Ratios
topics.description: Real-world applications of fractions, scaling, and proportional reasoning.
learning_objectives: Apply fraction concepts flexibly across various problem contexts.
Understand applications of fraction operations such as scaling.
Recognize how fractions function in ratio relationships.

topics.title: Early Equations and Expressions
topics.description: Foundational algebra including equivalence, equality, and substitution.
learning_objectives: Understand relational equivalence and that equations maintain status under algebraic manipulation.
Determine whether equations are true and solve equations using the four operations.
Follow the standard order of operations, including parentheses.

topics.title: Properties of Operations and Variables
topics.description: Understanding variables and algebraic properties in expressions.
learning_objectives: Apply the distributive property of multiplication over addition.
Interpret 'variable' as unknown quantities and quantities that vary connected to patterns.
Use less-than (<) and greater-than (>) symbols to compare quantities.

topics.title: Measurement Concepts and Tools
topics.description: Identifying attributes, selecting tools, and comparing measures.
learning_objectives: Describe and compare measurable attributes of objects.
Choose appropriate measurement tools and use them accurately.
Convert between measurement units within the same system.

topics.title: Calculating Perimeter, Area, and Volume
topics.description: Computing and estimating spatial measurements in problem solving.
learning_objectives: Calculate and estimate perimeter and area for two-dimensional shapes.
Calculate and estimate volume for solid figures.
Calculate and estimate measurements of angles in real-world problems.

topics.title: Geometry and Shape Attributes
topics.description: Classifying figures and understanding spatial relationships.
learning_objectives: Demonstrate understanding of shapes, attributes, and composing/decomposing shapes.
Draw shapes based on specific attributes like number of angles or equal faces.
Classify two-dimensional figures based on properties of lines, segments, rays, and angles.

sections.title: Science
sections.description: Essential content knowledge and specialized pedagogical understanding needed for teaching elementary science.

topics.title: The Universe and Its Stars
topics.description: Observable patterns in the sky and the relative distance of stars.
learning_objectives: Use observations of the Sun, Moon, and stars to describe predictable patterns.
Support arguments that the apparent brightness of the Sun and stars relates to relative distance from Earth.

topics.title: Earth and the Solar System
topics.description: Patterns of daylight, shadows, and seasons caused by Earth's motion.
learning_objectives: Relate the amount of daylight to the time of year using observations.
Represent data revealing patterns of shadows, day and night, and seasonal star appearances.

topics.title: History of Planet Earth
topics.description: Evidence of geologic history and slow versus fast planetary changes.
learning_objectives: Provide evidence that Earth events can occur quickly or slowly.
Identify evidence from rock formations and fossils to explain landscape changes over time.

topics.title: Earth's Materials and Systems
topics.description: Interactions between the geosphere, biosphere, hydrosphere, and atmosphere.
learning_objectives: Compare design solutions to slow or prevent wind/water from changing the land shape.
Make observations on weathering and erosion rates by water, ice, wind, or vegetation.
Develop models describing interactions among Earth's systems.

topics.title: Plate Tectonics and Water's Roles
topics.description: Large-scale system interactions, mapping Earth's features, and water distribution.
learning_objectives: Analyze data from maps to describe patterns of Earth's features and bodies of water.
Identify solid and liquid water locations on Earth.
Graph percentages of salt water and fresh water to show Earth's water distribution.

topics.title: Weather, Climate, and Biogeology
topics.description: Understanding weather patterns, climate regions, and organisms' effects on environments.
learning_objectives: Use observations to describe weather patterns over time and typical seasonal conditions.
Combine information to describe climates in different world regions.
Construct arguments for how plants and animals change environments to meet their needs.

topics.title: Earth and Human Activity
topics.description: Natural resources, severe weather hazards, and human impacts on Earth systems.
learning_objectives: Represent relationships between organism needs and living locations.
Make claims about design solutions reducing impacts of weather-related hazards.
Communicate solutions reducing human impact on land, water, air, and other living things.

topics.title: Plant and Animal Structures and Life Cycles
topics.description: External parts, survival structures, and diverse reproductive life cycles.
learning_objectives: Argue that internal and external structures function to support survival, behavior, and reproduction.
Determine patterns in parent/offspring behavior that help offspring survive.
Model that organisms have unique life cycles with common birth, growth, reproduction, and death.

topics.title: Matter and Energy Flow in Organisms
topics.description: How organisms obtain needs, process information, and utilize energy from the sun.
learning_objectives: Describe patterns of what plants and animals need to survive (air, water).
Model that energy in animals' food was once energy from the sun.
Model how animals receive, process, and respond to sensory information.

topics.title: Ecosystems and Energy Transfer
topics.description: Interdependent relationships, pollination, seed dispersal, and matter cycles.
learning_objectives: Investigate whether plants need sunlight and water to grow.
Mimic the function of an animal in dispersing seeds or pollinating plants.
Describe the movement of matter among plants, animals, decomposers, and the environment.

topics.title: Ecosystem Dynamics and Heredity
topics.description: Environmental changes, group behaviors, and inheritance/variation of traits.
learning_objectives: Make claims on solutions to problems caused when environment changes affect local organisms.
Argue that animals forming groups helps members survive.
Provide evidence that traits are inherited from parents but can be influenced by the environment.

topics.title: Biological Evolution and Diversity
topics.description: Fossil evidence, natural selection, adaptation, and comparative biodiversity.
learning_objectives: Interpret fossil data as evidence of ancient organisms and environments.
Explain how variations in characteristics provide survival/reproductive advantages.
Argue that some organisms survive well, less well, or not at all in particular habitats.

topics.title: Structure and Properties of Matter
topics.description: Classifying materials, disassembly, and understanding particles too small to be seen.
learning_objectives: Classify materials by observable properties and suitability for intended purposes.
Show that objects made of pieces can be disassembled and made into new objects.
Develop models describing matter as particles too small to be seen.

topics.title: Chemical Reactions and Conservation of Matter
topics.description: Reversible changes, conservation of weight, and formation of new substances.
learning_objectives: Argue that some changes caused by heating or cooling can be reversed and others cannot.
Graph quantities to show that total weight of matter is conserved during physical/chemical changes.
Investigate whether mixing two or more substances results in new substances.

topics.title: Forces, Motion, and Interactions
topics.description: Balanced/unbalanced forces, pushes/pulls, electricity, magnetism, and gravity.
learning_objectives: Compare effects of different strengths/directions of pushes and pulls on an object's motion.
Provide evidence of the effects of balanced and unbalanced forces.
Determine cause-and-effect for non-contact electric/magnetic interactions and gravitational force.

topics.title: Energy Conservation and Transfer
topics.description: Speed and energy, energy transfer through various means, and collision effects.
learning_objectives: Relate the speed of an object to the energy of that object.
Provide evidence that energy transfers from place to place via sound, light, heat, and electric currents.
Design, test, and refine devices that convert energy from one form to another.

topics.title: Waves and Electromagnetic Radiation
topics.description: Sound waves, light behavior, and information transfer technologies.
learning_objectives: Provide evidence that vibrating materials make sound and sound vibrates materials.
Develop models of waves describing amplitude, wavelength, and object movement.
Determine effects of placing transparent, translucent, opaque, or reflective objects in light beams.

topics.title: Engineering Design and Application
topics.description: Defining problems, creating solutions, and optimizing models or prototypes.
learning_objectives: Define simple design problems reflecting needs/wants, criteria, and constraints.
Generate and compare multiple possible solutions based on criteria.
Plan and carry out fair tests to identify aspects of a model that can be improved.

sections.title: Social Studies
sections.description: Essential content knowledge needed for teaching elementary social studies covering history, government, geography, and economics.

topics.title: Concepts of Chronology and Historical Sources
topics.description: Understanding time, interpreting timelines, and assessing historical sources.
learning_objectives: Create and interpret timelines and recognize change over time.
Identify primary and secondary sources and evaluate their validity and credibility.
Determine the appropriateness of sources for understanding historical events.

topics.title: Classical Civilizations
topics.description: Recognizing the major characteristics and contributions of ancient societies.
learning_objectives: Identify characteristics and contributions of China, Egypt, Greece, and Rome.
Make connections and comparisons among classical civilizations.
Interpret visual or written selections relating to classical civilizations.

topics.title: Indigenous Peoples of North America
topics.description: Characteristics of Native American cultures prior to European exploration.
learning_objectives: Identify numerous unique and well-developed Native American cultures in North America.
Understand interactions between Native Americans and Europeans.
Analyze early government decisions concerning Native Americans, including the Trail of Tears.

topics.title: European Exploration and Colonization
topics.description: Causes, patterns, and effects of European settlement in North America.
learning_objectives: Understand the timelines, settlement patterns, and reasons for exploration.
Identify major characteristics of the Thirteen Colonies and significant explorers.
Understand colonial culture, religion, and economy from various perspectives.

topics.title: American Revolution and Independence
topics.description: Conflicts between the colonies and Great Britain leading to independence.
learning_objectives: Identify the causes of the American Revolution.
Understand key individuals (Washington, Jefferson, Franklin) and their roles.
Analyze major ideas in the Declaration of Independence and their impacts.

topics.title: Development of the US Government
topics.description: The evolution of American governance and foundational documents.
learning_objectives: Understand major ideas in the Articles of Confederation.
Explain how the Constitution and Bill of Rights came into being, including compromises.
Understand the origin of political parties in the United States.

topics.title: Nineteenth Century Expansion and Conflict
topics.description: Westward expansion, slavery, and technological innovations before the Civil War.
learning_objectives: Understand Westward expansion, including the Louisiana Purchase and Lewis and Clark.
Analyze the origins and effects of slavery in the US Constitution and society.
Identify technological and agricultural innovations like the cotton gin and steamboat.

topics.title: Civil War and Reconstruction
topics.description: The Civil War era, abolitionism, and the impacts of Reconstruction.
learning_objectives: Examine economic and cultural differences between North and South, and abolitionist movements.
Identify key roles of Lincoln, Douglass, Tubman, and events leading to secession.
Understand the impacts of Reconstruction policies, amendments, and subsequent segregation.

topics.title: Late Nineteenth and Early Twentieth Century US History
topics.description: Industrialization, urbanization, business, and labor post-Civil War.
learning_objectives: Understand the roles of entrepreneurs like Carnegie and Rockefeller.
Analyze urban conditions, including living standards and child labor.
Identify waves of immigration from Europe in the late 19th and early 20th centuries.

topics.title: Twentieth and Twenty-First Century US History
topics.description: Major global conflicts, domestic movements, and economic shifts.
learning_objectives: Understand America's roles in WWI and WWII, and postwar consequences.
Analyze the Great Depression and the New Deal.
Examine the Civil Rights movement, Women's suffrage, and Information Age impacts.

topics.title: Concepts of Family, Community, and Government
topics.description: Roles, responsibilities, and relationships between levels of government.
learning_objectives: Understand the concepts of family and community.
Compare the major responsibilities of local, state, and federal governments.
Understand the relationship between state and federal government systems.

topics.title: Forms of Government and Responsible Citizenship
topics.description: Differentiating government types and understanding citizens' rights/duties.
learning_objectives: Differentiate among forms of government like democracy, monarchy, and oligarchy.
Understand separation of powers and the system of checks and balances.
Identify the rights of democratic citizens and their legal obligations (e.g., jury duty, taxes).

topics.title: Physical Geography and Location
topics.description: Mapping, interpreting geographic features, and understanding Earth's natural systems.
learning_objectives: Read and interpret physical, topographical, and political maps.
Use longitude, latitude, equator, and international dateline for location.
Identify geographic features and understand short- and long-term physical changes.

topics.title: Human-Environment Interaction
topics.description: How humans adapt to, utilize, and alter physical environments.
learning_objectives: Analyze population trends, immigration patterns, and settlement patterns.
Understand how environments impact essentials like food, shelter, and industry.
Assess the effects of human-initiated changes (pollution, deforestation, global warming).

topics.title: Fundamental Economic Concepts
topics.description: Basic understanding of needs, wants, goods, services, and market forces.
learning_objectives: Explain how human needs are met through production, trading, and exchange.
Understand scarcity, cost, opportunity cost, and specialization.
Describe the basic principles of supply and demand.

topics.title: Earning, Spending, Saving, and Businesses
topics.description: Personal finance basics, banks, and the roles of entrepreneurs.
learning_objectives: Explain the relationship between work, earning, spending, and saving money.
Understand the basic services that banks and financial institutions provide.
Describe the role of an entrepreneur and interactions between individuals, businesses, and government.

topics.title: Patterns of Economic Activities
topics.description: US and global macroeconomic patterns, taxation, and trade.
learning_objectives: Identify reasons governments levy taxes and the national debt.
Understand the government's role in currency and the Federal Reserve System.
Recognize major trade relationships and distinctions between developing and developed nations.
